county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dubuque County formed 1834 from Original county (Michigan Territory). County seat is Dubuque.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Dubuque holds deeds from 1834; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Dubuque County is one of Iowa's two original counties and home to the oldest European settlement in Iowa -- Julien Dubuque mined lead here from 1788. The city of Dubuque has the strongest Irish Catholic community in Iowa, with records at the Archdiocese of Dubuque archives. Lead mining records from the 1830s-1860s document early workers. Loras College and Clarke University archives. National Mississippi River Museum. Center for Dubuque History at Loras College is the premier local repository. German immigrant community records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Dubuque County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Dubuque
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1836 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Dubuque
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1836. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Dubuque / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1836. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Dubuque County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Dubuque County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Dubuque County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Dubuque County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Dubuque County Auditor, Dubuque
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Dubuque County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Early Church Records -- Mississippi River Settlements
source_profiles.repository: Various -- diocesan archives, denominational archives, FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Church registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) from early Mississippi River settlements in Dubuque County. Catholic, Lutheran, Methodist, and Presbyterian records. German and Irish congregations predominate.

source_profiles.source_name: Dubuque County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: State Historical Society of Iowa / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Dubuque County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, land sale notices. State Historical Society of Iowa holds microfilm of many Iowa newspapers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Emmet County formed 1851 from Dickinson, Kossuth. County seat is Estherville.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Estherville holds deeds from 1859; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Emmet County was settled in the 1860s-1870s, delayed by the Spirit Lake Massacre aftermath. Estherville is known for the 1879 Estherville meteorite. Emmet County Historical Society. Scandinavian (Norwegian and Swedish) settlers predominate. Armstrong and Ringsted communities. Iowa Lakes Corridor. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online. Des Moines River headwaters area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Emmet County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fayette County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is West Union.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in West Union holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Fayette County was settled by New Englanders and Germans. Upper Iowa University in Fayette (est. 1857) holds institutional and regional records. West Union newspaper archives from the 1850s. Fayette County Historical Society. Oelwein railroad community -- Chicago Great Western Railway records. Volga River valley. Clermont and Elgin communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Fayette County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Floyd County formed 1851 from Chickasaw. County seat is Charles City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Charles City holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Floyd County is centered on Charles City, long associated with the Oliver Corporation (Hart-Parr tractor company, later Oliver Farm Equipment). Company employment records document worker families. Floyd County Historical Society. German and Scandinavian settlers. Rockford and Nora Springs communities. Cedar River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Floyd County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Franklin County formed 1851 from Chickasaw. County seat is Hampton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Hampton holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Franklin County was settled in the 1870s after railroad development. Hampton was the birthplace of the 4-H movement in Iowa -- agricultural extension records. Franklin County Historical Society. Strong German and Scandinavian heritage. Sheffield and Coulter communities. West Fork of Cedar River. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Franklin County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fremont County formed 1847 from Pottawattamie. County seat is Sidney.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sidney holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Fremont County in southwestern Iowa was part of the Mormon Trail corridor. Tabor was a noted Underground Railroad and Free-State Kansas activist community -- John Brown connections. Tabor College records. Fremont County Historical Society. Sidney and Hamburg river communities on the Missouri River floodplain. Strong Congregationalist church records in Tabor. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch microfilm.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Fremont County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1847. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Mormon Trail Records -- Fremont County Area
source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Latter-day Saint emigrant records, pioneer journals, and camp records for the Fremont County area of the Mormon Trail across Iowa (1846-1869). Includes both LDS members and non-Mormon settlers who arrived along the same corridor.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Greene County formed 1851 from Dallas. County seat is Jefferson.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Jefferson holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Greene County is known as the 'Bell Tower of Iowa' for its many church steeples. Jefferson's courthouse square features a unique bell tower. Mahanay Memorial Carillon Tower. Greene County Historical Society. Lincoln Highway heritage -- Jefferson was a key stop on the transcontinental highway. Scranton and Grand Junction communities. Raccoon River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Greene County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Grundy County formed 1851 from Black Hawk. County seat is Grundy Center.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Grundy Center holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Grundy County was settled predominantly by German and Dutch immigrants. Strong German Reformed and Dutch Reformed church records. Herbert Quick, Iowa novelist, was from Grundy Center. Grundy County Historical Society. Reinbeck and Wellsburg communities with German heritage. Black Hawk Creek valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Grundy County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Guthrie County formed 1851 from Jackson (renamed from Panora). County seat is Guthrie Center.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Guthrie Center holds deeds from 1852; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Guthrie County was settled by American-born migrants in the 1850s. Guthrie Center is the hometown setting of 'Arlo Guthrie's Restaurant' fame. Springbrook State Park area early settlement. Panora and Stuart communities. Guthrie County Historical Society. Middle Raccoon River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online. The county was briefly named Panora before being renamed Guthrie.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Guthrie County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Guthrie County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Guthrie County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Guthrie County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Guthrie County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Guthrie County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Guthrie County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Guthrie County Auditor, Guthrie Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Guthrie County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hamilton County formed 1856 from Webster. County seat is Webster City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Webster City holds deeds from 1857; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Hamilton County was settled after the railroad arrived in the late 1850s. Webster City was a manufacturing center. Boone River valley settlements. Hamilton County Historical Society. Stratford and Stanhope communities. Scandinavian and German settlers. Duncombe and the Kendall Young Library hold local materials. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Hamilton County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Webster City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1856 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Webster City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1856. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Webster City / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1856. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Hamilton County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Hamilton County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Hamilton County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Hamilton County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Hamilton County Auditor, Webster City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Hamilton County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Hamilton County Scandinavian Church Records -- Lutheran Congregations
source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for Hamilton County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.
